commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Through many tribulations. Our daily offences require the paternal chastisement of the Almighty. The concupiscence of the flesh too, which wills against the spirit, must be subdued by punishment. Woe then to you, lovers of this world, who wish to pass your lives without tribulation, enemies of the cross. Senseless creatures, is the disciple above his master? Did it not become Christ first to suffer, and thus to enter into his glory? and shall we pretend to enter by any other means? (Denis the Carthusian)

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: And when they had preached the gospel to that city, and had taught many, they returned again to Lystra, and to Iconium, and Antioch, giving way to them while their passions are roused, but when they have ceased, then attacking them again. Do you mark, that it was not by (supernatural) grace that they managed all that they did, but by their own diligence. Confirming, it says the souls of the disciples: ἐ πιστηρίζοντες, further establishing; so that they were established, but they added more thereto. And that we must, etc.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
